target.name: Lightmatter
target.founder: Nicholas Harris, Darius Bunandar, Thomas Graham

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $420M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Lightmatter is a Series C-stage semiconductor company that has raised $420M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 total comparables in the Chips & Semiconductors sector: 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including d-Matrix, DEEPX, Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, and PsiQuantum.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series C forward step-up multiplier of 1.7x to the $420M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $714M. Peer-median analysis examines 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Etched ($5.0B, Series B), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), d-Matrix ($2.0B, Series C), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C). The median valuation across this peer set is $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B.

The two methods produce a triangulated valuation band of $607M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The wide spread reflects genuine dispersion in recent semiconductor fintech valuations and stage heterogeneity within the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the durability of the peer valuations in an evolving AI chip market and Lightmatter's specific differentiation relative to competitors in optical and photonic computing approaches.
